objective: Repair a trust and functionality gap by giving Profile a real browser-local JSON export flow, then push and deploy with proof.
truth_boundary: This proof covers browser-local demo data controls only.
WHOOP, account sync, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, backend writes, and shared-context daily usability are not claimed.
The LifeMax lab preflight remained red because the local MCP/shared-context fetch did not load life safety or Grok context state.
defects_found: Profile exposed a local export preview but did not provide a real download or copy workflow, so the data-control surface looked more like explanatory copy than functional privacy tooling.
The first export smoke script used top-level await with CommonJS require syntax and had to be rerun inside an async wrapper.
The first desktop export smoke reloaded with the export details collapsed, so saved-plan proof was not visible until the preview was opened before capture.
A concurrent build/screenshot command collided with another Next build lock; the build and screenshot commands passed after rerunning sequentially.

repairs_made.summary: Added LocalDemoExport, createLocalDemoExport, and serializeLocalDemoExport with an explicit browser-local truth boundary.
Added Profile Download local JSON, Copy JSON, export status messaging, and a structured local export preview.
Added compact data action and export status styling.
Added unit coverage proving the export schema and boundary text.
Added Playwright coverage for Profile export download, export preview schema, saved-plan content, and mobile screenshot proof.

git.commit_subject: feat: add local data export controls
git.push_status: origin/main matched local main after push

commands_run.summary: Fixture contract tests passed: 1 file, 2 tests.
PWA manifest test passed: 1 file, 1 test.

commands_run.command: npm run build && npm run screenshot:mobile
commands_run.summary: Production build passed; screenshot/mobile Playwright suite passed after sequential rerun.
No value-shaped credential patterns were found in changed or untracked public files.
Linked Vercel project production deployment reached READY after explicit production approval.
Omega public production alias was READY at deployment dpl_3bM4rKBUDtue23QK3VWp1oeyVEFD.

omega_mobile.status_message: Download prepared. The file contains only this browser's local demo data.

omega_desktop.status_message: Download prepared. The file contains only this browser's local demo data.

loop_evidence.stop_continue_decision: checkpoint, not complete: the public browser-local demo is more functional and more credible, but real backend/shared-context daily usability remains blocked by the red lab preflight and unconnected integrations.

residual_risks: The app is still a browser-local demo, not the full LifeMax dogfood product.
Copy JSON depends on browser clipboard availability; download was the primary browser-smoked export path.
Full-page screenshots can show the sticky mobile shell in the page body because of capture mechanics; viewport checks did not find horizontal overflow.
Preview deployment access may still be affected by Vercel protection; public proof used the omega production alias.
No credential, auth, DNS, backend, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, or WHOOP changes were made.
next_action: Continue with project/preview protection hygiene or start a separate backend/shared-context Wave 1 repair once the lab MCP boot path is green.
